tax.guideIntro: Summary based on 2026 NTS and income tax law. Confirm actual filing with a tax professional.
tax.profileDesc: Select your situation to see applicable taxes and estimated amounts from holdings and trades.
tax.investorGeneralDesc: KRX gains tax-exempt for non-major shareholders
tax.investorMajorDesc: Market cap 5B+ KRW or 1%+ stake — gains taxed
tax.isaSectionDesc: ISA net income is calculated separately with tax-free limits and 9.9% separate taxation. If trades are not tagged ISA/regular, income is split by ratio.
tax.isaIncomeShareDesc: Share of dividends, trades, and other financial income in ISA (direct input below takes priority)
tax.pensionSectionDesc: Employee pension savings — 6M KRW annual limit, 13.2% tax credit estimate
tax.otherFinancialIncomeDesc: Deposit interest, bond interest, etc. (excluding dividends)
tax.otherIncomeBracketDesc: For comprehensive income tax brackets — select your bracket
tax.applicableDesc: {{count}} tax items based on your selections · See full reference in
tax.noTransactions: No sales or dividends in {{year}}. Register trades, dividends, splits, or mergers to see estimates.
tax.guideLinkHint: For full tax rules, foreign dividend withholding, and brackets, see

estimate.basis: Based on registered trades & dividends · FX
estimate.disclaimerForeignGain: Foreign stock capital gains must be reported and paid May 1–31 of the following year.
estimate.disclaimerComprehensive: If financial income exceeds 20M KRW, comprehensive income tax filing is required in May.

guide.bulletFinancialTax: Financial investment income tax is not in effect as of 2026 (under review/repeal)
guide.bulletForeignGain: Foreign stock capital gains — report May 1–31 next year
guide.bulletComprehensive: Financial income (interest + dividends) over 20M KRW triggers comprehensive taxation
guide.shelterAccountsDesc: ISA net income after account-level netting gets a tax-free limit; overflow is taxed at 9.9%. ISA-taxed income is excluded from the 20M KRW comprehensive threshold.
guide.pensionCreditDesc: Pension savings contributions qualify for a 13.2% tax credit (6M KRW annual limit). Shown separately from dividend/trading taxes in this app.
guide.capitalDeductionDesc: Foreign stocks and major shareholder gains — 2.5M KRW annual deduction (once for domestic + foreign combined). General investors' KRX listed gains are tax-exempt.
guide.incomeTaxBracketsDesc: When financial income exceeds 20M KRW, all interest and dividends are combined with other income and taxed at the brackets below (+ 10% local tax).

kr-capital-general.title: General investor trading gains
kr-capital-general.summary: KRX listed gains tax-exempt for non-major shareholders
notes: Financial investment income tax not in effect as of 2026
Major shareholders and unlisted stocks taxed separately

kr-capital-major.title: Major shareholder gains
kr-capital-major.summary: Capital gains tax when market cap ≥ 5B KRW or stake ≥ 1%
notes: Domestic and foreign gains can be netted (from 2020.1.1)
Expenses such as fees require documentation

kr-dividend.title: Dividend income (separate taxation)
kr-dividend.thresholdLabel: Separate taxation if financial income ≤ 20M KRW
kr-dividend.filingLabel: Comprehensive filing in May if over 20M KRW
notes: Deposit interest, bond interest, and all dividends combined
Over threshold: all financial income taxed progressively (6–45% + local)

kr-securities-tax.title: Securities transaction tax
kr-securities-tax.summary: Levied on sale proceeds (small amount on buys too)

us-capital.title: Foreign stock trading gains
us-capital.thresholdLabel: Basic deduction 2.5M KRW/year (once for domestic + foreign)
notes: Foreign stock gains/losses netted within the year
FX: FIFO or trade-date rate (this app uses a single rate estimate)

us-dividend.title: Foreign dividend income
us-dividend.summary: Local withholding + additional KR tax per treaty
us-dividend.thresholdLabel: May filing recommended even if financial income ≤ 20M KRW
notes: Separate taxation final if ≤20M KRW and only domestic withholding
Foreign dividends must be reported regardless of withholding (for credit)

financial-comprehensive.title: Comprehensive financial income tax
financial-comprehensive.summary: Progressive tax when interest + dividends exceed threshold
notes: All financial income taxed when over threshold

isa-account.title: ISA (Individual Savings Account)
isa-account.summary: Account-level netting, tax-free limit, 9.9% on overflow
notes: Stocks, ETFs, funds, deposits combined; 3-year minimum
ISA separate tax excluded from 20M comprehensive threshold
Securities transaction tax still applies separately

pension-savings.title: Pension savings contribution credit
pension-savings.summary: Tax credit on employee pension contributions (13.2% estimate)
notes: IRP and retirement pension have separate combined limits
Separate from dividend/trading taxes — contribution credit

general.label: ISA general
general.description: 2M KRW net income tax-free, 9.9% on overflow

worker.label: ISA worker/farmer
worker.description: 4M KRW tax-free if income requirements met

US.note: KR–US treaty — no additional KR tax (May filing for foreign tax credit)

us-capital.conditional: Major shareholder — netted with domestic major shareholder gains
us-capital.applies: Foreign trading gains — 2.5M KRW deduction then 22%

financial-comprehensive.applies: Financial income over 20M KRW — progressive tax
financial-comprehensive.conditional: Financial income ≤ 20M KRW — separate taxation maintained

isa-account.applies: Tax-free up to {{limit}}M KRW net income, 9.9% on overflow

pension-savings.conditional: Enter contribution amount to estimate tax credit

kr-gain-major.label: Major shareholder capital gains (domestic + foreign netted)

disclaimers.0: This estimate is for reference only. Confirm actual tax and filing obligations with NTS or a tax professional.
disclaimers.1: Foreign stock FX conversion uses a single rate (current or input). Actual filing uses trade-date rates and FIFO.
disclaimers.2: Securities transaction tax, major shareholder rules, and expenses (commissions) are omitted or simplified.
disclaimers.3: Financial investment income tax (2026: not in effect) is not included.
disclaimers.4: ISA is estimated via account-level netting and income share split. Actual settlement is at financial institution maturity.

headline.noCapital: Set invested capital to start personalized allocation suggestions

description.underweight: {{region}} allocation is {{gap}}%p below target. See buy suggestions below.
description.overweight: {{region}} allocation is {{gap}}%p above target. See buy suggestions below.
description.balanced: Market and holding weights are near target. Review concentrated positions and cash allocation.

reason.trimWeight: Weight {{weight}}% → consider trimming to {{target}}% or below
reason.trimUnitTooSmall: Overweight but below sell unit — watch
reason.reserveCash: Remaining cash after adjustment — for additional buys or emergency reserve

sentiment.headline: {{market}} featured stocks · {{sentiment}}
sentiment.noData: Insufficient quote data to assess sentiment.

description.strong_bull: Featured stocks avg {{avg}}%. Up {{up}} · Down {{down}} — strong short-term uptrend.
description.bull: Featured stocks avg {{avg}}%. Up {{up}} · Down {{down}} — mild bullish tone.
description.neutral: Featured stocks avg {{avg}}%. Up {{up}} · Down {{down}} — no clear direction.
description.bear: Featured stocks avg {{avg}}%. Up {{up}} · Down {{down}} — correction/selling pressure.
description.strong_bear: Featured stocks avg {{avg}}%. Up {{up}} · Down {{down}} — broad weakness.

recommendation.momentumStrong: Strongest mover among {{market}} featured stocks today — check trend continuation.
recommendation.bullWatchlist: In bull market, {{name}} — watch relative strength.
recommendation.bearPullback: Large pullback among {{market}} featured stocks — watch oversold signals.
recommendation.bearDefensive: Relatively resilient {{name}} in bear market — defensive interest.
recommendation.neutralMomentum: Relative strength for {{name}} in mixed market — confirm direction.
recommendation.neutralWatchlist: {{name}} pulling back in mixed market — watch support.

reasoning.bullish: When rising names clearly outnumber fallers, risk appetite tends to be strong. Broad participation is healthier than a few large caps alone.
reasoning.bearish: When fallers dominate, short-term selling and risk-off sentiment may be elevated. Check whether the index holds while leaders weaken.
reasoning.neutral: Mixed up/down moves suggest no clear direction — stock and sector differentiation matters more than trend-following.

indexTrend.title: {{name}} — {{trend}}
indexTrend.reasoning: Moving-average alignment is a common trend filter. Price above short/medium MAs in bullish order suggests uptrend; the opposite suggests pullback or weakness.

indexRsi.title: {{name}} RSI(14) {{rsi}} — {{zone}}
indexRsi.reasoning: RSI(14) measures relative strength over 14 sessions. Above 70 is often overbought, below 30 oversold — but strong trends can stay extreme; do not use RSI alone as a signal.

indexMacd.title: {{name}} MACD {{side}}{{alignedSuffix}}
indexMacd.reasoning: MACD tracks 12/26 EMA momentum. Above zero with price above SMA20 often reads as bullish momentum; below zero suggests weakening momentum.

indexBb.title: {{name}} Bollinger (20,2) — {{label}}
indexBb.reasoning: Bollinger bands are SMA20 ± 2σ. %B near 1 suggests upper-band breakout (overheated); near 0 suggests lower band (oversold). Squeeze then expansion often precedes volatility spikes.

indexStoch.title: {{name}} Stochastic — {{label}} (K {{k}} / D {{d}})
indexStoch.summary: Uses %K/%D cross and 80/20 zones for short-term momentum.
indexStoch.reasoning: Stochastic measures close vs recent range. Above 80 overbought, below 20 oversold; K crossing above D is a common short-term buy signal (often fails in strong trends).

reasoning.vix: VIX is the S&P 500 implied-vol fear gauge — widely quoted on terminals and financial media.
reasoning.fx: USD/KRW affects foreign flows, exporters, imports, and growth names. Sharp KRW weakness can also signal higher volatility.
reasoning.yield: US Treasury yields reflect discount rates, growth expectations, and Fed policy. Curve spreads (e.g. 10Y–3M inversion) are on recession watchlists.

sectorOverview.title: {{region}} sectors — 1W RS leader {{sector}}
sectorOverview.reasoning: Sector ETF relative strength is excess return vs the benchmark. Judge whether leadership reflects rotation or a trap before chasing laggards.

newsEmpty.title: News headlines unavailable
newsEmpty.summary: Could not fetch news feeds. Use the links below.
newsEmpty.reasoning: News APIs/RSS may fail temporarily or when keys are missing. Cross-check headlines with prices and indicators.

news.title: {{region}} headlines — {{toneLabel}}
news.reasoning: Headline keywords (surge, drop, concern, etc.) give a rough tone scan. Flow across sources matters more than one article; prices may already reflect the news.

macroGlobal.reasoningDiverge: Large gaps in featured-stock averages may reflect FX, rates, or regional themes — often read alongside USD/KRW.
macroGlobal.reasoningSync: Similar direction in both markets suggests a common global driver — cross-check Fed, FX, and macro headlines.

reasoning.momentum: Strong daily/short-term returns attract trend followers — confirm volume and relative strength vs the index.
reasoning.pullback: Sharp drops are where bounce buyers and trend exits disagree — check support, RSI oversold, and news.
reasoning.defensive: Names holding up in weak markets often have lower volatility or solid fundamentals — they may lag on rebounds.
reasoning.watchlist: In mixed markets, individual catalysts (relative strength, dividends, earnings) matter more.

evidence.newsFinnhubSkip: US Finnhub news skipped when Finnhub key is not set
evidence.newsRssDelay: Google News RSS may be delayed depending on network

vix.extremeFearDetail: VIX 30+ often appears in panic selloffs. High vol itself — distinguish dead-cat bounce vs continued trend.
vix.elevatedFearDetail: More hedging and selling demand — pressure on risk assets may increase.
vix.normalDetail: Near historical average (~15–20). No strong fear/greed signal.
vix.lowVolDetail: Market in complacency mode — very low VIX can warn of reversal for some traders.

fx.krwWeakDetail: ~{{rate}} KRW/USD — weak won often helps exporters/foreign flows but weighs on imports, debt, and growth names.
fx.krwStrongDetail: Strong won — supportive for liquidity/imports but may constrain exports and foreign buying.
fx.stableDetail: FX moves are limited — macro impact from rates is modest.

yield.risingDetail: {{name}} yield rising — tighter financial conditions and pressure on growth valuations. Watch Fed and inflation expectations.
yield.fallingDetail: Lower yields can support risk-on/growth but may overlap with slowdown concerns.
yield.highRegimeDetail: {{yield}}% — 2020s higher-rate regime; rising discount rates weigh on long-duration growth multiples.
